metadata.title: A Battery Can Be Full and Still Crash Your Device

beats.narration_text: Hello. This is Liam, in for Bear. The coin cell had most of its charge left, and the device browned out and rebooted anyway.

props.command: claude "write brownout.py: CR2032 (3V nominal, R_internal limiting ~3mA). Given an inference current pulse, model V = V_oc - I*R over time and the brown-out reset line."
props.segment: A Battery Can Be Full and Still Crash Your Device
output: Add 100uF cap: sag flattens → no brownout

beats.narration_text: From the textbook: the annotated current trace. Our brownout detector looks for exactly the pattern in this figure — a peak current spike that dips the rail below the reset threshold.

beats.act: MANIM — Voltage trace: sag below reset threshold
beats.narration_text: The CR2032 sits at 3 volts open-circuit, but its internal resistance is about 35 ohms. Peak inference current: 10 milliamps. Terminal voltage equals 3 volts minus 10 milliamps times 35 ohms: 2.65 volts. The processor's brownout reset fires at 2.7 volts. The trace dips below the line. Reboot.

beats.act: MANIM — Bulk cap added: sag flattens
beats.narration_text: Add a 100 microfarad bulk capacitor in parallel. The voltage sag during a 20-millisecond inference pulse: delta-V equals current times time divided by capacitance — 10 milliamps times 20 milliseconds divided by 100 microfarads — 2 volts absorbed. The terminal voltage stays above 2.7. No reboot.

beats.act: MANIM — Energy vs peak: two different questions
beats.narration_text: Two different questions. How long will it run? That is an energy question — multiply capacity times voltage times efficiency. Will it run at all? That is a peak question — multiply peak current times internal resistance and check the sag against the reset threshold. A full battery can fail the second question.

Your turn. Ask Claude: write brownout.py — model a CR2032 with 3 volt open-circuit voltage and internal resistance limiting to about 3 milliamps. Given an inference current pulse, model V equals V open-circuit minus I times R over time, and overlay the brownout reset line. Run it to simulate the sag without any hardware. Then add a second run with a 100 microfarad bulk cap and show the sag flatten — that is what prevents the brownout.

props.command: claude "write brownout.py: CR2032 (3V nominal, R_internal limiting ~3mA). Given an inference current pulse, model V = V_oc - I*R over time and the brown-out reset line."

props.title: A Battery Can Be Full and Still Crash Your Device
